beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Andre starts every day with a swim, but first handles morning basics. Full energy to start the week; sunny weather ahead.

beats.activity: Early-morning swim to start the day.
beats.reasoning: Can't start the day without a swim; early time ensures he's back home before heading to work, and the sunny forecast makes this appealing.

beats.activity: Return home, breakfast, and prep for work shift.
beats.reasoning: Refuel after swim and gather gear for 24-hour shift; travel buffer from Shoreline to Tukwila.

beats.activity: Drive to fire station in Tukwila.
beats.reasoning: Across-town commute (Shoreline to Tukwila); starts 24-hour shift; Light Rail closure is irrelevant (driving solo).

beats.activity: Start 24-hour fire station shift; equipment check and station duties.
beats.reasoning: Firefighter on 24-hour rotation; morning routine includes vehicle and equipment checks before potential calls.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA match on station TV with crew during shift break.
beats.reasoning: USA vs Wales match airs at 11am; Andre is risk-averse and prefers close friends over crowds, so watching at the station with crew fits better than heading to a downtown bar. Captures the match at the scheduled time.
Fire station meal; typical crew lunch routine during a 24-hour shift.

beats.activity: Continue 24-hour shift; respond to calls and station maintenance.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon portion of 24-hour shift; unpredictable but station-based as baseline. Sunny weather may reduce call volume.

beats.activity: Dinner and evening downtime at station.
beats.reasoning: Typical crew dinner during evening hours of 24-hour shift; social time with coworkers (close friends he values).

beats.activity: Late evening shift work and station duties.
beats.reasoning: Final hours of 24-hour shift; evening call response and station wrap-up before heading home.
End of 24-hour shift; return to Shoreline home. Across-town drive (Tukwila to Shoreline) with evening traffic.

beats.activity: Wind down and sleep at home.
beats.reasoning: After a full 24-hour shift, Andre sleeps at home; 7+ hours of rest spanning into early Tuesday morning.

diary: Started the day right with an early swim at Shoreline Pool—water was perfect, cleared my head before the shift. The drive to Tukwila was smooth, caught the station just as the crew was firing up coffee. We got through equipment checks without incident, then gathered around the TV for the USA-Wales match at 11. The energy was good; everyone was invested, even between calls. Had a couple of runs in the afternoon—nothing major, just keeps you sharp. Made a decent dinner around 3, the kind of meal that sticks with you through a long night. The evening quieted down nicely. Drove home as the sun was still up, feeling that good tired that comes after a solid 24. Looking forward to a real bed.
thought_process: The swim was intentional—I'm 60 now, and I need that routine to keep the body ready for the physical demands of the job. The World Cup match was a nice morale boost for the crew; we don't get many moments like that where everyone just relaxes together. I skipped heading downtown to the bars despite the early openings—the Light Rail closure didn't affect us anyway, and honestly, after 24 hours I just want quiet. The calls today were routine, which I'm grateful for. I noticed my mood lifted when we had that break together watching the match. It's the small things that make the long shifts bearable.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
